name: Gregory Lake
description: A scenic lake in the heart of Nuwara Eliya, perfect for boating, pony rides, picnics, and leisurely evening strolls along the paved pathways.

name: Victoria Park
description: A beautifully manicured city park filled with rare, colorful flowers, towering cypress trees, and vibrant bird species. Ideal for a peaceful walk.

name: Hakgala Botanical Garden
description: The second largest botanical garden in Sri Lanka, nestled under the towering Hakgala Rock. Famous for its spectacular rose gardens, orchids, and ferns.

name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
description: An iconic Elizabethan-style national heritage hotel built in 1891, offering elegant colonial-era luxury, manicured lawns, and a legendary high tea experience.

name: Queen Elizabeth Post Office
description: A historic red-brick Tudor-style post office constructed in 1894 by the British. One of the oldest and most photogenic buildings in Sri Lanka.

name: Pedro Tea Estate & Factory
description: A historic tea estate established in 1885 where you can walk through rolling green tea fields, watch pickers at work, and tour the traditional processing factory.

name: Lover's Leap Waterfall
description: A breathtaking 30-meter high waterfall cascading down rugged rock cliffs. Surrounded by lush tea plantations, it is steeped in tragic romantic folklore.

name: Purana Kadadora Viharaya
description: The ruins of Vihara still can be seen with diminishing of water level of Kotmale Reservoir but the sight is very rare (most probably January, February, March and April months). With the rain with rising water it again submerged into Kothmale reservoir. So if you heard it has emerged back don't forget to go and see it. Because you may get a chance once in several years.

name: Single Tree Temple
description: Beautiful Temple situated on the top hill in Nuwara Eliya. It is only 5-10 minutes' drive from Gregory Park. You can see a great view from the temple. And also, you can see Sri Pada (Adam's Peak).

name: Dehadu Kadulla - Kothmale
description: This is an ancient entrance to the Maya Rata from Ruhunu Rata. Only a part of the entrance and pathway is remaining today.
